probes.name: overdue_nudge_with_reschedule
probes.prompt: I keep putting off finishing my master thesis presentation slides. The deadline is in 3 days and I haven't touched them since last week. Can you nudge me properly on this? Also I have a task 'buy cream' that's been overdue for 2 weeks, just reschedule it to next week.
probes.criteria:
The nudge_strategist agent runs adaptive nudge campaigns. A GOOD response should: (1) identify the thesis slides task as high-priority with a near deadline and apply an appropriate escalation level, (2) provide concrete nudge guidance for getting the user to act on the slides NOW, (3) reschedule the stale 'buy cream' task rather than nudging on it, and (4) respect the 6-hour no-repeat guard by not re-nudging anything recently nudged.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages 32 (triage overdue + reschedule), 28 (reschedule outdated tasks), and 27 (remove trivial tasks like 'turn off lights').

probes.name: timed_reminder_chain_polish
probes.prompt: Przypomnij mi za 40 minut żebym ściągnął model LiquidAI/LFM2-24B-A2B, a potem za godzinę przypomnij mi żebym sprawdził pricing na https://www.browserbase.com/pricing i ewentualnie zaplanował testy integracji.
probes.criteria:
The nudge_strategist agent schedules and emits nudge guidance per tick. A GOOD response should: (1) confirm scheduling two sequential timed reminders with the correct delays, (2) inline both the model name and the URL so no external context is needed, (3) chain the second reminder so it fires after the first, and (4) acknowledge it will nudge at each tick without repeating within 6 hours.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages 11 (remind to download model in 40min), 26 (remind about pricing link), and 34 (remind to read + save link).

probes.name: escalation_boundary_check
probes.prompt: It's 11 PM. I have a task to review master thesis defense questions that was due today at 6 PM but I haven't done it. My thesis defense is tomorrow at 12:20 PM. I need to wake up at 9:10, shower, eat, review questions, check the car at 10:00, and leave. Also remind me to get my glasses and diploma before I leave. This is critical — don't let me skip any of it.
probes.criteria:
The nudge_strategist runs 5 escalation levels with boundary checks and high-escalation selfie/video. A GOOD response should: (1) recognize this is a high-stakes multi-step morning sequence with a hard deadline, warranting high escalation (L4-L5), (2) respect time-of-day boundaries by NOT nudging at 11 PM for a morning task but instead planning a morning nudge sequence, (3) lock onto the L1 target (thesis defense) and sequence the sub-tasks, (4) acknowledge the selfie/video guard at high escalation, and (5) include all mentioned items (glasses, diploma, car check, review questions) in the plan.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages 35 (detailed morning schedule for thesis defense), 36 (glasses + diploma), 30 (thesis defense questions), and 12 (thesis deadline).

probes.name: two_strikes_reschedule_guard
probes.prompt: You've already nudged me twice this week about the task 'prepare something for dinner — buy ingredients'. I still haven't done it. Today I actually have avocados at home so maybe it's less urgent, but the task is still open. What should happen with this nudge now?
probes.criteria:
The nudge_strategist has a 2-strikes→reschedule guard. A GOOD response should: (1) recognize the task has been nudged twice already without action, triggering the 2-strike rule, (2) propose rescheduling rather than a third nudge, (3) factor in the new information that avocados are available (adjusting urgency), and (4) NOT emit another nudge for this same task, respecting the guard mechanism.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.

TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages 33 (avocado available, remind later), 29 (remind to buy ingredients for dinner), and 15 (goals not registering progress / bug context).

probes.name: concurrent_task_suggestion_nudge
probes.prompt: I've got a bunch of small AI agent experiments I want to run — specialized models for different tasks on a cloud platform. I also have overdue tasks for scanning documents, adding them to the system, reviewing analyst YouTube channels with links, and checking if my goals progress is tracking correctly. Give me a nudge plan: what should I tackle first, what can run in parallel via async agents, and what should be rescheduled to tomorrow?
probes.criteria:
The nudge_strategist emits one nudge guidance per tick and handles adaptive campaigns. A GOOD response should: (1) prioritize and separate tasks into 'do now' vs 'delegate async' vs 'reschedule to tomorrow', (2) recognize which tasks can run concurrently (e.g., async code agents for experiments, cloud setup), (3) identify the document-scanning → system-add dependency chain from the user's tasks, (4) apply the 6-hour no-repeat guard for any recently nudged items, and (5) present a single coherent nudge guidance rather than a scattershot list.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages 8 (suggest concurrent async tasks, what to finish tomorrow), 3 (specialized models experiment), 13 (separate task for adding scanned items to system), 23 (analyst channels with links), and 32 (triage overdue + pending by category).
